turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm has completely collapsed: diversity (8.66e-16, normalized 4.56e-18) and velocity (mean 7.54e-17, zero-velocity ratio 1.0) are effectively zero. All particles have virtually identical fitness (~110.11), which is far from the global optimum (0). The swarm has been stalled with no improvement for 200 iterations. No boundary violations or velocity clipping; particles are frozen at an interior point, not at edges. | [DIAGNOSE] The root cause is classic premature convergence to a suboptimal point on the Rosenbrock function. The swarm likely entered the narrow, curved valley and lost momentum, eventually collapsing to a single point where the gradient is very small, causing a complete diversity and velocity collapse. The absence of exploration pressure has trapped the swarm far from the global minimum. | [STRATEGIZE] A radical injection of diversity is needed to escape this local basin. Resetting a large fraction of the worst-performing particles to random positions across the search space will restore exploration while preserving the best solution (poor as it is). Accompanied by a higher inertia weight, this should restart the search and enable the swarm to discover better regions. A milder perturbation (Gaussian or Lévy) would likely be insufficient given the extreme convergence.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset of 15 worst particles (50% of swarm) introduced entirely new random positions and velocities, and the inertia weight of 1.1 encourages continued exploration. The best cost remained unchanged because the global best was not affected; this is expected. The swarm now has high diversity and should be able to explore new regions of the search space. The diagnosis of extreme premature convergence was correct, and the radical reset was an appropriate response. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] The Rosenbrock function likely has multiple deceptive local minima with long, sharp valleys. After the reset, the swarm should escape the previously converged basin, but we need to watch for signs of renewed convergence to other suboptimal points. The root cause of the initial failure (insufficient exploration pressure leading to velocity collapse) has been temporarily resolved. Future interventions may need to balance exploration and exploitation as the swarm approaches the basin of the true optimum.
